title: Evangelion 02 post Harpy attack
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion 02 post Harpy attack             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Fri Jan 08, 2010 11:50 pm What we see here is the body of Evangelion Unit 02 after her battle against the mass production Evangelion. Now my question is.. Is she truly dead, The Eva that is. I know they can rebuild the Eva unit since they rebuild 00 after the many times she blew up. Did the MPE rip the core unit from Eva 02 or was it still intact after the battle was over, I assume the units ripped it from her to stop full function of the Eva. We see them carrying different parts of the Eva but we never see the core. Does the Eva truly have all those organs within them, I know they are fully living beings but I've always wondered if they had full organs. They may have them but they may not function as they are not really needed.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Jan 08, 2010 11:54 pm I made this post in the "Unable to Help" thread regarding Asuka's death, but it does go with the death of her Eva and includes details on the core. Sachi wrote: Now we all know that when the first lance penetrated Unit-02 through the eye, Asuka's actual eye began profusely bleeding, as if you were pouring water out of a cup. This alone is enough to kill her quite quickly if left unattended. I'd post a screenshot, but my DVD isn't the clearest, so the blood would be hard to see between the red background and her red Plugsuit. When Unit-02 goes berserk as Asuka raises her hand to the sky, a single lance is thrown, splitting her arm in two: We don't see bleeding yet, since we only view it for a quick glimpse, but it's safe to assume that there was a lot more blood along with the loss of her arm. This may not kill her right away, but, like with her eye, if left unattended she will bleed to death. And if you thought that wasn't over kill, what would seven more lances do to her? And we're not even done yet; the harpies continue to torture her: Each of the Mass Production Evangelions had their turns eating her Eva with her inside it (possibly still synched). And if you look closely, can you see a core anywhere? The Entry Plugs are plugged in directly to the core and the lack of one here suggests two things: 1) The core was ripped out and tossed to the side from several hundred feet in the air (not a likely chance of surviving unscathed) or 2) The core was eaten, with the entry plug and Asuka attached. Impossible to survive, in my opinion. So after all that, between getting speared several times and eaten, I think it's quite safe to say that Asuka is dead, very dead.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Sat Jan 09, 2010 12:02 am Great post Sachi! Now, we see that Unit 02 has been pretty much destroyed in every way shape and form but again NERV can always rebuild her but can they rebuild "her". If the core was destroyed which we assume is a yes then the soul should have left it and thus the Eva is just a bunch of lumps of flesh and meat. Something else to point out, when we see Asuka within her plug we can see day light and we know that the core is inside the Eva so that makes me assume that the whole front has been ripped out and since the plug goes down into the core itself then to see day light the core would have had to have been removed yet the Eva functions...Hmmm..

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Jan 09, 2010 12:11 am Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: Great post Sachi! Now, we see that Unit 02 has been pretty much destroyed in every way shape and form but again NERV can always rebuild her but can they rebuild "her". If the core was destroyed which we assume is a yes then the soul should have left it and thus the Eva is just a bunch of lumps of flesh and meat.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Sat Jan 09, 2010 12:16 am that is true, forgot about the whole -eyes of the Eva- thing, dang..Wonder if she sees more cause Unit 02 has 4 eyes. Did they have to Rebuild 00 completely since well it exploded?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Jan 09, 2010 12:19 am Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: Did they have to Rebuild 00 completely since well it exploded?

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Sat Jan 09, 2010 2:48 am Sachi wrote: Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: Did they have to Rebuild 00 completely since well it exploded?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Jan 09, 2010 2:50 am Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: Then what about Eva 00 when it exploded, it was still called Unit 00.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Jan 09, 2010 2:52 am Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: Then what about Eva 00 when it exploded, it was still called Unit 00.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Sat Jan 09, 2010 2:53 am Sachi wrote: Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: Then what about Eva 00 when it exploded, it was still called Unit 00.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Jan 09, 2010 3:18 am Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: I thought that in EOE they secured it.

[]:             Quote Post by jayngfet » Sat Jan 09, 2010 3:55 am An evangelion is essentially an angel clone. In one early episode we hear it's impossible to do lasting damage to an angel without striking the core. It stands to reason that the same is true of an EVA. Meaning since the core was still around they could feasibly rebuild unit 02. It'd help if there were usable organs but I'm not sure organs are wholly necessary(angels get on fine without them. It's prolly just to help synchronization).

[]:             Quote Post by Agentomega » Sat Jan 09, 2010 3:58 am They secured "the Purple Unit" as I recall... Pretty sure that they mention Zerogouki is gone in the beginning somewhere...

[]: Re: Evangelion 02 post Harpy attack             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sat Jan 09, 2010 7:59 am Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: Is she truly dead, The Eva that is.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Jan 09, 2010 12:16 pm In terms of Kyoko's soul being put into a new Eva, it's unknown whether she just plain died during the battle or was in Instrumentality but somehow can't return. (Considering that one extra-canon card line of ALL humans were drawn to Instrumentality.) The whole "anybody can return" line is a pain in the ass. What of those who died in 2I or before? Or people like Kaji who died shortly before 3I? Can only people who died in 3I return?? And/or do souls--like Kyoko--count?

[]:             Quote Post by Killericon » Sat Jan 09, 2010 12:35 pm This flash from EoE settles it pretty definitively for me, or am I wrong in assuming that it's not foreshadowing, and just Asuka's fears?

[]:             Quote Post by Starasp » Sat Jan 09, 2010 12:37 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: In terms of Kyoko's soul being put into a new Eva, it's unknown whether she just plain died during the battle or was in Instrumentality but somehow can't return. (Considering that one extra-canon card line of ALL humans were drawn to Instrumentality.) The whole "anybody can return" line is a pain in the ass. What of those who died in 2I or before? Or people like Kaji who died shortly before 3I? Can only people who died in 3I return?? And/or do souls--like Kyoko--count?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Jan 09, 2010 12:58 pm I meant on the day 3I happened, which would at least qualify Asuka. But Kyoko... It's just so confusing if former-people who were souls at the point of 3I can reform their own bodies once Instrumentality ended or what. There's also the consideration that Kyoko had her Insane post-CE AND sane/maternal aspects of her soul together inside Eva-02 by the time the series occured. (Sorry if this veering off-topic, but all of it's confusing as hell. )

[]:             Quote Post by Starasp » Sat Jan 09, 2010 1:15 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: I meant on the day 3I happened, which would at least qualify Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Sat Jan 09, 2010 2:10 pm That is to say that Asuka's mom even remembers what she looked like before starting her new life inside the Eva.

Article: Evangelion 02 post Harpy attack
're not even done yet; the harpies continue to torture her: Each of the Mass Production Evangelions had their turns eating her Eva with her inside it (possibly still synched). And if you look closely, can you see a core anywhere? The Entry Plugs are plugged in directly to the core and the lack of one here suggests two things: 1) The core was ripped out and tossed to the side from several hundred feet in the air (not a likely chance of surviving unscathed) or 2) The core was eaten, with the entry plug and Asuka attached. Impossible to survive, in my opinion. So after all that, between getting speared several times and eaten, I think it's quite safe to say that Asuka is dead, very dead.
